raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need find .php files recursively, count lines per file, sum. Use find, wc -l, awk. Command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l | awk 'END{print "Total:", $1}'? But wc -l prints per file and total. Actually wc -l on multiple files prints each and total. So just: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l. That gives per file and total. If want only total: use awk. But requirement: counts lines in each file and prints total count as well. So wc -l is fine. Provide command.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: Need find .php files recursively, count lines per file, sum. Use find, wc -l, awk. Command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l | awk 'END{print "Total:", $1}'? But wc -l prints per file and total. Actually wc -l on multiple files prints each and total. So just: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l. That gives per file and total. If want only total: use awk. But requirement: counts lines in each file and prints total count as well. So wc -l is fine. Provide command.
